target.title: fix: gracefully handle broken provider modules in media understanding registry
target.body:
## Problem

When a bundled provider module (e.g. codex) has unresolved imports in Nix builds — specifically `Cannot find package 'openclaw'` imported from `codex/provider.js` — the call to `resolvePluginCapabilityProviders()` in `buildMediaUnderstandingRegistry()` throws, which kills the **entire** media understanding registry. This makes ALL image analysis fail, even for unrelated providers like Google Gemini that would work fine on their own.

## Root Cause

`buildMediaUnderstandingRegistry()` calls `resolvePluginCapabilityProviders()` eagerly with no error handling. A single broken bundled provider module crashes the registry build before any providers can be registered. The fallback chain in `runWithImageModelFallback` then has zero candidates to try.

## Fix

Wrap `resolvePluginCapabilityProviders()` in a `try/catch` in `provider-registry.ts`:
- On success: normal behavior, all plugin providers loaded
- On failure: log a warning via subsystem logger, continue with an empty plugin provider list
- Config-based providers (from `resolveImageCapableConfigProviderIds`) are still registered as fallback

This means that even if codex is broken, Google Gemini (or any other image-capable model in config) still works.

## Changes

- `src/media-understanding/provider-registry.ts` — try/catch around `resolvePluginCapabilityProviders()`, with structured warning log
- `src/media-understanding/provider-registry.test.ts` — added test for graceful degradation (all 6 tests pass: 5 existing + 1 new)

## Testing

```
 ✓ |media-understanding| provider-registry.test.ts (6 tests) 48ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  6 passed (6)
```

Verified on a Nix-built deployment where codex has a broken `import 'openclaw'` — before the fix, all image tool calls failed. After the fix, image analysis works via config-based providers.

## Related

- Related to #66702 (image contamination — different bug but same code area)
- The underlying codex Nix build issue (`Cannot find package 'openclaw'`) is a separate packaging problem; this fix makes the registry resilient to it

- Edge cases checked:
  - Existing send paths without metadata remain covered by tests.
  - Generated protocol schema and Swift models remain in sync.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Live production deployment.
  - Maintainer CI environment beyond local targeted checks.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  Metadata shape may be ignored by older consumers.
  - Mitigation:
    Metadata propagation is opt-in and existing behavior remains backward compatible.

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `448d4813e8f`: fix the plugin-name compatibility on the REAL plugin path (addresses ClawSweeper [P1]).** The previous fix preserved a same-named plugin only at the final gateway deny filter, but the production plugin loader filters `pluginToolDenylist` names first — so a same-named plugin `read`/`write`/`edit` was still dropped before that filter (the bot was right; the prior unit proof passed only because the mocked `createOpenClawTools` ignored the denylist). Fix: in `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`, the host-FS coding names are now excluded from the `pluginToolDenylist` handed to the loader **when they are denied only by the host-FS default** (explicit `gateway.tools.deny` / owner-only still deny the plugin); the built-in stays gated at the final filter. The test mock now **mirrors the production loader** (drops a plugin-owned tool whose name is in `pluginToolDenylist`), so the regression tests exercise the real path. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container — **red/green**: reverting the exclusion makes the two plugin-compat tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`; with the fix they pass. The real resolver output was confirmed directly (the `pluginToolDenylist` it passes excludes `read`/`write`/`edit` in the host-FS-default case, includes a name under explicit `gateway.tools.deny`). Full gate green: `tools-invoke-http` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http` 19/19, `schema.help.quality` 23/23, `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` clean, **`tsgo:core` typecheck clean** (also fixed pre-existing `defaultGatewayDeny`/`ownerOnlyGatewayDeny` `.includes(string)` type errors my earlier `tsc` check had missed), oxlint clean.

---

> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `228ade8544e`: rebased the full stack onto current `main`.** Conflict resolved (`scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` surface budgets reconciled to main's current values + this stack's deltas; `config-types` deprecated budget 416→417 for the new public `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` type). The plugin-compat [P1] fix (source-aware host-FS default-deny) survives the rebase and is **re-proven against current main**: in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container, reverting only the resolver fix makes the two plugin-compat tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`, and restoring it passes — so the regression exists on current main and the fix removes it. Full gate green on the rebased head: `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` 19/19, `schema.help.quality.test.ts` 23/23, `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` clean, oxlint clean, typecheck clean. Now `MERGEABLE` against main. The host-FS write boundary still needs explicit maintainer/security acceptance (a product decision, not an author fix).

---

> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `acb9ab0a39d`: fix ClawSweeper [P1] plugin-name compatibility.** Adding `read`/`write`/`edit` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` name-denied any *same-named plugin tool* over `/tools/invoke` + SDK `tools.invoke`, so a pre-existing non-optional plugin `read`/`write`/`edit` returned 404 even with the host-FS opt-ins off. Per the resolver, the default-deny is now **source-aware** (`src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` `HOST_FS_BUILTIN_CODING_DENY_NAMES` + the gateway deny filter in `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): a same-named tool whose *only* deny reason is the host-FS built-in coding default is preserved when it carries plugin metadata (`getPluginToolMeta`); the built-in coding tool (no plugin meta) stays gated behind the owner gate + opt-in, and owner-only / explicit `gateway.tools.deny` / `exec`/`fs_write`/etc. are unaffected. Two regression tests assert a plugin `read`/`write` stays reachable **without** a new `gateway.tools.allow` entry. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container: `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` 19/19, `schema.help.quality.test.ts` 23/23; oxlint clean. Red/green proof: reverting only the resolver fix makes both new tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`. **Still pending: rebase onto current `main`** (main refactored the gateway deny-handling path, so the rebase needs careful re-expression — tracked separately).

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `4fe6f873c9` (scope owner gate to built-in host-FS write tools; restacked on #85664 `d625c6de23`).** Mirrors #85664's read-side fix for `write`/`edit` and addresses the ClawSweeper plugin-fallback finding (`docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` write section). Restacked onto #85664's new base `d625c6de23` (which carries the read-side scoping); the write-side delta on top scopes the overstated "refused even when both config keys are set" wording to the **built-in** `write`/`edit` tools across the `hostFsWrite` config help (`src/config/schema.help.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` member comment (`src/config/types.gateway.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` bullet in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and the write section of `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` (prose, truth table, _Plugin name collision_ note). Per the resolver (`src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): for a non-owner caller the built-in is never materialized, but a same-named allowlisted plugin tool still resolves under the normal `gateway.tools.allow`/`deny` policy. Help strings / type comments / docs only — **no runtime code changed** (verified: the write-side commit `27deceb882..4fe6f873c9` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/types.gateway.ts`, and the two `docs/gateway/*.md` files — no `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, or `schema.labels.ts`), so the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `pnpm check:docs` (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links) and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `61e6f29bed` (config-help owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]/[P3]).** Restacked onto #85664's new base `7f9876c2b2`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `661d6838c0` is **config help / type docs** (verified: `git diff 661d6838c0 61e6f29bed` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts` +4 and `src/config/types.gateway.ts` +25 — comment/string lines; every runtime file under `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, `schema.labels.ts` is byte-identical): the `hostFsWrite` `FIELD_HELP` string and the `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary + `hostFsWrite` member comment now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces, and the type summary is refreshed so it no longer calls `hostFsWrite` "future work". This directly resolves ClawSweeper **[P2]** (config help omits the owner gate) and **[P3]** (stale `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary). Because no runtime code changed, the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) and `pnpm check:docs` both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `661d6838c0` (docs owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]).** Restacked onto #85664's docs-updated read base `ca46eb9946`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `afe36ccf45` is **docs**: the `read` + `write`/`edit` truth tables in `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` and the `hostFsRead`/`hostFsWrite` bullets in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces — directly resolving ClawSweeper's **[P2]** ("Document the owner gate for file-tool opt-ins"). No source files changed (verified: `git diff afe36ccf45 661d6838c0` touches only those two docs files), so `dist/` is byte-identical and the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. `pnpm check:docs` passes in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links).

---

Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the `resolveGatewayScopedTools` resolver). Same **triple-key owner-gated** shape as #85664's `read`: a surface-specific class opt-in **plus** the per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` **plus** `senderIsOwner === true`, all on the `http` surface.

## Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base (head `afe36ccf456f`, base upstream `023ce6e96c38`)

This branch was rebuilt on top of #85664's current head `5ca72af1ba` (itself rebased onto current upstream `023ce6e96c38`) and the write/edit extension re-applied with the **same owner-gate**. This directly resolves the prior ClawSweeper [P1].

**Bot [P1] (resolved):** upstream commit `02182d5a3031` ("refactor: remove sender owner tool gating") deleted the `ownerOnly` enforcement helper, so the earlier `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag this PR added became dead code — a trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller could have reached an allowlisted mutating tool with no owner check. **Fix:** this PR no longer relies on that tag. `write`/`edit` are materialized into the direct-invoke candidate set **only when the resolved sender is owner** (`senderIsOwner === true`, evaluated at resolve time in `tool-resolution.ts`), exactly mirroring #85664's read gate. A trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller is now refused even with both config keys set (proven below, cases 2 & 4).

## Triple-key gating

To reach a write-class tool on the direct-invoke surface, ALL must hold:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. | `false` |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` includes the tool name | Per-tool enablement. | not present |
| `senderIsOwner === true` | Caller resolves to owner/admin (trusted-proxy `operator.admin`, or shared-secret bearer). | — |

Truth table (tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`, surface = `http`):

| `allow` ∋ `T` | `hostFsWrite` | sender owner | `T` reachable |
|---|---|---|---|
| no  | no  | —   | ❌ |
| yes | no  | yes | ❌ (class opt-in off; built-in not materialized; `dangerous_allow` audit fires) |
| no  | yes | yes | ❌ (not in allow) |
| yes | yes | **no**  | ❌ (**owner gate** — refused) |
| yes | yes | yes | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (behind `hostFsWrite: true` + allow + owner).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` + allow + owner.
- **`apply_patch`:** present in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for defense-in-depth, but the coding-tools factory produces no `apply_patch` entry, so it is never materialized (proven below, case 7).
- **NOT exposed:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` — RCE-class; out of scope for this PR.

## Audit

- `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` fires only when BOTH `hostFsWrite: true` AND a write-class name is in `allow` (mirrors `host_read_allow`); escalates to `critical` on non-loopback bind.
- `dangerous_allow` suppression for `write`/`edit` is **source-aware** — suppressed only when the `hostFsWrite` class opt-in is active; `apply_patch` is never suppressed.

## Files changed

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — `hostFsWrite` schema entry
- `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — field help + label
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — owner-gated write/edit materialization; coding built-ins win plugin name collision when class opt-in active
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `host_write_allow` finding; source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression
- `src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — write coding-tool collision precedence (owner→built-in, non-owner→plugin, opt-in-off→plugin)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — `host_write_allow` dual-key + source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression cases (incl. `apply_patch` never suppressed)
- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` — coding-tool factory plumbing
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md`, `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — `hostFsWrite` opt-in, triple-key truth tables, `host_write_allow` catalog row

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Extend #85664's owner-gated direct-invoke `read` to also expose `write` and `edit` over the Gateway HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke` surfaces, behind the same triple key (class opt-in `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` + per-tool `gateway.tools.allow` + `senderIsOwner`). Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base so write/edit materialize only for an owner sender — resolving the prior [P1] where upstream `02182d5a3031` made the old `ownerOnly` tag dead code.

**Real environment tested:** Built fresh from head `afe36ccf456f` inside a `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container (full runtime build → `dist/index.js`; SDK → `packages/sdk/dist/index.mjs`). Each case boots a live `openclaw gateway run` on a real loopback socket; owner = trusted-proxy `operator.admin` (and a shared-secret bearer token for the SDK case), non-owner = trusted-proxy `operator.write`. Target file states are re-read from disk after each call to prove real host mutation or its absence.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:** `PROOF_HEAD=afe36ccf456f bash .proof-63919.sh` inside the container — boots nine gateway configs and dispatches `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` over HTTP `/tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke`, asserting the HTTP status and re-reading each target file from disk.

**Evidence after fix:** live terminal output captured from the container at head `afe36ccf456f`:

```
=== HEAD: afe36ccf456f ===

### LIVE PROOF (HTTP trusted-proxy surface) ###

--- 1 W-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 24 bytes to /work/.proof63/w1.txt"}]}}
  file=OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f
--- 2 W-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=PRESEED
--- 3 E-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /work/.proof63/e3.txt."}],"details":{"diff":" 1 ALPHA\\n-2 BETA\\n+2 GAMMA-afe36ccf456f", ...}}}
  file=ALPHA / GAMMA-afe36ccf456f
--- 4 E-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: edit"}}
  file=ALPHA / BETA  (unchanged)
--- 5 W-DENIED-no-opt-in (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=false, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 6 W-DENIED-no-allow (allow=[] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 7 APPLYPATCH-denied (allow=[apply_patch] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=apply_patch) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: apply_patch"}}
--- 8 READ-REGRESSION (allow=[read] hostFsRead=true, operator.admin, tool=read) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"READ-BASE-OK-afe36ccf456f\\n"}]}}

### LIVE PROOF (SDK token surface — shared-secret bearer = owner) ###

--- 9 SDK-W-OWNER (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, token bearer, tool=write) ---
  [SDK token] {"ok":true,"toolName":"write","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 28 bytes to /work/.proof63/w9.txt"}]},"source":"core"}
  file=SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f

=== PROOF COMPLETE: HEAD=afe36ccf456f ===
```

Independent re-read of persisted file states after the run: `w1.txt`=`OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`, `w2.txt`=`PRESEED` (non-owner write left it untouched), `e3.txt`=`ALPHA/GAMMA-afe36ccf456f`, `e4.txt`=`ALPHA/BETA` (non-owner edit left it untouched), `w5.txt` and `w6.txt` absent (never created), `w9.txt`=`SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`.

**Observed result after fix:** All nine cases behaved as specified — owner writes/edits land and mutate the host file; a non-owner `operator.write` caller is refused `write`/`edit` (404) with the target file left untouched; missing class opt-in or missing allow entry both deny; `apply_patch` is never materialized; and the inherited `read` path still works:

| # | Case | Config | Caller | HTTP/SDK | File state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | write owner | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 wrote 24 bytes | written |
| 2 | write **non-owner** | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 3 | edit owner | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 replaced 1 block | edited |
| 4 | edit **non-owner** | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 5 | write no opt-in | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=false | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 6 | write no allow | allow=[], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 7 | apply_patch | allow=[apply_patch], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | factory never materializes it |
| 8 | read regression | allow=[read], hostFsRead=true | `operator.admin` | 200 + contents | #85664 base intact |
| 9 | write owner (SDK) | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | token bearer | ok=true wrote 28 bytes | written |

Supplemental (not a substitute for the live proof above), all in the same `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container at head `afe36ccf456f`: unit tests — **160 passed** across the gateway test projects (`tools-invoke-http` 42 + `tool-resolution` 5 + `audit-gateway-tools-http` 19, ×3 projects = 7 file-projects); `lint` (oxlint, 0/0), `tsgo:core` (0), `check:test-types` (0), and `plugin-sdk-surface-report` vitest (4/4). **`checks-node-core-fast` / plugin-SDK surface budget:** this PR adds `hostFsWrite?` as a *field* on the existing `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` type — **zero new top-level exports** — so `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` passes on the `+1` budget already carried by #85664's base commit (`config-types` 416 / public 10285 / public-fn 5164 / deprecated 3238). No further budget change in this PR.

**Not tested:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` (RCE-class; out of scope for this PR and never exposed by the direct-invoke factory).

target.title: fix: Codex startup plugins + WhatsApp history & Docker Codex OAuth
target.body:
## Summary

Two related fixes for **Docker + Codex** operation and **WhatsApp** agents:

1. **Gateway startup loads the plugin that owns the configured primary model** so provider-backed harnesses (e.g. Codex) register **before** channels start handling traffic. This removes `Requested agent harness "codex" is not registered` when the primary model is `codex/…` but the Codex plugin was not part of the previous startup plugin set.

2. **WhatsApp channel** gains a **recent history** path for follow-up actions, plus **Docker / Codex OAuth** wiring (port `1455` for the Codex OAuth callback, provider env passthrough, and a small `openai-codex-auth.sh` helper) so the stack matches documented Codex subscription flows.

## Motivation

- **Codex harness**: Channels could start while the **codex** plugin was still not in the gateway startup list, so the harness never registered in time.
- **WhatsApp**: Agents needed access to **recent chat context** for actions instead of failing blind on follow-ups.
- **Docker**: Codex OAuth (pi-ai callback on `127.0.0.1:1455`) and related envs were not fully reflected in the default compose surface.

## Changes

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.ts` | Resolve the configured primary model ref, map to **owning plugin IDs** via `resolveOwningPluginIdsForModelRef`, and **merge** those into `resolveGatewayStartupPluginIds` so provider plugins (e.g. codex) load at startup when implied by config. |
| `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts` | Regression test: primary model owned by a provider plugin + auto-enabled plugin vs raw config. |
| `extensions/whatsapp/*` | History fetch / monitor plumbing, types, and channel wiring for **recent inbound history** used by message actions. |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Publish **1455:1455** for Codex OAuth callback; document intent in comments. |
| `scripts/docker/openai-codex-auth.sh` | Small helper for Codex auth flow in Docker. |
| Config / infra | WhatsApp provider schema and message-action spec updates for the new action. |

## How to test

1. **Codex / harness**: Configure primary model `codex/gpt-5.4` (or equivalent) with embedded Codex harness; start gateway. Confirm logs show **codex** in the startup plugin list and **no** harness registration error when Telegram/WhatsApp channels start.
2. **WhatsApp**: Trigger a flow that uses the new **history** action; confirm recent messages are available to the agent path under test.
3. **Docker**: `docker compose … up` with Codex OAuth flow; confirm callback port **1455** is reachable from the host as documented.

## Risk / rollout

- Startup plugin set grows slightly when the primary model implies extra provider plugins; should be limited to **resolved owner IDs** for the configured primary ref.
- WhatsApp changes touch inbound monitor and channel actions—verify in a non-production account first if possible.

## Checklist

- [x] Unit test added for gateway startup plugin resolution (`channel-plugin-ids.test.ts`).
- [ ] Maintainer review for WhatsApp extension surface area.

target.title: docs: clarify `/reset` is not an alias for `/new`
target.body:
Fixes #76592.

This updates the slash command docs and native command summaries so `/new` and `/reset` are no longer presented as interchangeable aliases.

- `/new [model]` starts a new session.
- `/reset` resets the current session in place.

The old wording called `/reset` the reset alias, which made it sound interchangeable with `/new`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The PR removes user-facing alias-like wording for `/new` and `/reset`. After this patch, the docs say `/new` starts a new session and `/reset` resets the current session in place; ACP/native command summaries now say the same thing.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 26.3.1 OpenClaw runtime host with OpenClaw `2026.5.3-1 (2eae30e)` available, plus this PR branch checked out locally at `893e25f76e285c74b37a79cac2b579aa6936bc90`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran terminal commands against the patched branch to read the exact user-facing strings that changed:

```text
sed -n '125,128p' docs/tools/slash-commands.md
sed -n '23,24p' src/acp/commands.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the patched branch:

```text
$ sed -n '125,128p' docs/tools/slash-commands.md
    - `/new [model]` starts a new session. `/reset` resets the current session in place; it is not an alias for `/new`.
    - Control UI intercepts typed `/new` to create and switch to a fresh dashboard session; typed `/reset` still runs the Gateway's in-place reset.

$ sed -n '23,24p' src/acp/commands.ts
  { name: "reset", description: "Reset the current session in place." },

```

**What was not tested:** No additional gaps.

This is a docs/user-facing command-copy fix only. It does not change command dispatch behavior.

## Testing

- Ran `git diff --check`.
- Verified the updated docs line and ACP command summaries with the terminal output shown above.
- Did not run `pnpm build`, `pnpm check`, or `pnpm test` because this changes only documentation and command descriptions.

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex to locate the contradictory wording, prepare the docs patch, and align the related command summaries after review feedback.

target.title: fix(feishu): normalize reaction message IDs for API calls
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #34528 by separating Feishu synthetic reaction event IDs from Open Message IDs used for Feishu API calls.
- Adds `normalizeFeishuOpenMessageId` and applies it to replies, reads, edits, card updates, media replies, reactions, pins, and streaming-card replies.
- Adds regression coverage for reaction-suffixed reply targets, lookups, and edits.

## Background
Feishu reaction notifications use synthetic OpenClaw message IDs like `om_xxx:reaction:THUMBSUP:<uuid>` so reaction turns remain unique. Those IDs were later passed back into Feishu endpoints that only accept the base `open_message_id`, causing 400 responses such as `Invalid ids: [om_xxx:reaction:...]`.

## Verification
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/feishu/src/message-id.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts extensions/feishu/src/pins.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.ts extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/feishu/src/message-id.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts extensions/feishu/src/pins.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.ts extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed` (typecheck passed; lint failed on pre-existing unrelated `extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts` switch exhaustiveness after untracked local workspace directories forced all lanes)

## Impact
Reaction events still keep unique synthetic IDs inside OpenClaw, while Feishu API calls now use valid base Open Message IDs. This prevents reaction-triggered reads/replies/actions from failing with Feishu invalid ID errors.

target.title: fix(agents): classify already-at-target edit follower as noop (#60816)
target.body:
### Summary

Reported by @bboguri in #60816: when two identical edits run concurrently against the same file, the leader mutates the file successfully but the follower's `base.execute(...)` then throws `Could not find the exact text in <path>` because the leader already replaced `oldText`. The wrapped recovery layer in `src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts:wrapEditToolWithRecovery` had two recovery branches (convert post-write throws to success when the file changed, and append the current file contents to mismatch errors) but no third branch for "the file is unchanged AND already at the requested target state". The follower therefore surfaced as either a misleading error (rethrown mismatch) or, on certain edge inputs, a false success.

clawsweeper-bot's review on this issue prescribed the exact fix shape:

> Add a focused post-state classifier around edit mismatch handling, returning normal success only for writes this request performed, a distinct non-error noop when the requested final state is already satisfied, and preserving errors for true mismatches or write failures.

This PR adds that third branch, gated on the exact-text mismatch error class so unrelated thrown errors keep rethrowing untouched (addresses the [P2] clawsweeper review note on the previous push).

### Fix

`src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts`:

1. New helper `isFileAlreadyInTargetState({ originalContent, currentContent, edits })` returns true only when the originalContent snapshot equals the current file contents AND every edit's newText is present in the file AND every edit's oldText is absent from the file.
2. New helper `buildEditNoopResult(pathParam)` returns the prescribed copy `Edit noop: content already up to date in <path>.` distinct from the existing `Successfully replaced text in <path>.` used by the post-write recovery branch.
3. `wrapEditToolWithRecovery` consults the noop classifier inside the existing `shouldAddMismatchHint(err)` block, after `didEditLikelyApply` (so genuine post-write throws still report success) and before `appendMismatchHint` (so a false-positive mismatch on a follower no longer re-throws). Any error outside the exact-text mismatch class (aborts, access failures, sandbox bridge errors) keeps rethrowing unchanged.

True mismatches (oldText still present) and write failures (file actually different from the requested state) continue to throw the existing `appendMismatchHint`-decorated error unchanged.

### Tests

`src/agents/pi-tools.read.host-edit-recovery.test.ts`:

- Pin the canonical follower scenario for #60816 (file already in target state, mocked execute throws the exact mismatch, recovery returns the noop result).
- Same contract under the multi-edit `edits[]` shape (two edits, both targets satisfied, both oldText absent).
- Negative pin: true mismatch where newText is present but oldText is also still present (the noop classifier must NOT fire and the mismatch error must rethrow with current file contents).
- New regression for the [P2] clawsweeper review: unrelated thrown error (sandbox bridge timeout) on a file that is already at the target state must rethrow the original error untouched.

### Changelog

Added one bullet under `## Unreleased` -> `### Fixes` in `CHANGELOG.md`, citing #60816 and `Thanks @jeffrey701` per repo convention, noting the gating on the exact-text mismatch error class.

### Note on unrelated CI red

`check-test-types` and `check` are red on this PR head, but every failure is in `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-tts.test.ts` (lines 125-198, `'reply' is possibly 'undefined'` and similar). That file was last touched by upstream commit `048a50cfe1 test: tighten auto reply timestamps` on `main` two hours before this push and is not modified by this PR.

Fixes #60816.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Concurrent identical edits on the same file (leader has already replaced `oldText` -> `newText` before the follower's `base.execute(...)` runs). Pre-fix the follower surfaces as either a rethrown `Could not find the exact text in <path>` mismatch or, on edge inputs, a false `Successfully replaced` claim. After this patch the follower is classified as `Edit noop: content already up to date in <path>.`, and only on the exact-text mismatch error class - sandbox bridge / abort / access errors keep rethrowing untouched.

Real environment tested: Linux 6.17.0-23-generic, Node.js v22.22.0, Bash terminal, this PR head `c3b613fdba` checked out. The reproducer below imports `wrapEditToolWithRecovery` directly from `src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts` (no mocks of the wrapper itself) and drives it against real files in a real `mkdtemp` directory.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the reproducer below from a fresh shell after checkout of `c3b613fdba`. Command:

    cd /path/to/openclaw
    node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/77638-repro.mts

Reproducer source (`/tmp/77638-repro.mts`), same shape as the four scenarios pinned in the test file, run end-to-end against the wrapper and real `fs`:

    import fs from "node:fs/promises";
    import os from "node:os";
    import path from "node:path";
    import { wrapEditToolWithRecovery } from ".../src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts";

    const tmpDir = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "openclaw-77638-repro-"));

    function makeTool(opts) {
      const base = { name: "edit", execute: async () => opts.exec() };
      return wrapEditToolWithRecovery(base, {
        root: tmpDir,
        readFile: (p) => fs.readFile(p, "utf-8"),
      });
    }

    // Scenario A: follower already at target - leader replaced "old text" -> "new text"
    // Scenario B: non-mismatch error (sandbox bridge timeout) at target state
    // Scenario C: true mismatch (oldText still present alongside newText)
    // Scenario D: post-write throw with real file change

Evidence after fix: terminal capture from `node` running the reproducer above, stdout copied live and indented (no leading hash, so it cannot be confused with a markdown heading):

    tmpDir = /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q

    Scenario A: follower already at target -> Edit noop
      isError=false
      text="Edit noop: content already up to date in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo1.txt."
      -> OK

    Scenario B: non-mismatch error at target -> rethrows untouched
      -> THREW: Sandbox bridge timed out before edit could run

    Scenario C: true mismatch (oldText still present) -> hint rethrow
      -> THREW: Could not find the exact text in demo3.txt. The old text must match exactly including all whitespace and newlines.
    Current file contents:
    old text and new text both here

    Scenario D: post-write throw with real file change -> Successfully replaced
      isError=false
      text="Successfully replaced text in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo4.txt."
      -> OK

    repro complete

Observed result after fix: Scenario A (canonical #60816 follower) returned `isError: false` with content text `Edit noop: content already up to date in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo1.txt.` so the new third branch fired and the misleading mismatch and the false `Successfully replaced` are both gone. Scenario B (the [P2] regression) rethrew `Sandbox bridge timed out before edit could run` verbatim because the noop classifier did NOT fire (underlying error was not the exact-text mismatch class) even though the file was at the target state. Scenario C rethrew `Could not find the exact text in demo3.txt.` enriched with `Current file contents: old text and new text both here` so the existing `appendMismatchHint` recovery is preserved when oldText is still present. Scenario D returned `Successfully replaced text in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo4.txt.` so the existing `didEditLikelyApply` recovery still fires.

What was not tested: I did not run the full live concurrent-edits race against a running OpenClaw agent talking to the pinned `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent@0.73.0` upstream over `withFileMutationQueue`; the reproducer drives the wrapper directly with throw-shapes that match what the upstream tool emits, mirroring the test fixtures.

target.title: fix(ui): format structured tool results instead of "[object Object]
target.body:
## Problem
The Control UI could render structured tool output as `[object Object]` when `toolresult` blocks carried non-string `text` / `content`, or when stream-synthesized messages stored raw object values.
## Change
- Format object-shaped `toolresult.text` and object-shaped non-array `content` with the existing `formatUnknownText(..., { pretty: true })`.
- Normalize streamed tool output through `formatToolOutput(entry.output)`.
- Preserve empty-string outputs by avoiding truthy checks that drop `""`.
## Tests
- Added coverage for object-shaped `toolresult.text`.
- Added coverage for object-shaped stream results.
- Verified synthesized message content contains stringified JSON, not `[object Object]`.
## Verification
- `cd ui && npm run build` passed.
- `cd ui && npm test` ran unit tests successfully, but the full command exited non-zero because Playwright Chromium is not installed in this local environment.
## Local test note
Vitest’s Playwright project could not launch Chromium from `~/.cache/ms-playwright`. This appears to be an environment issue, not a failure in the updated code or assertions.
## Maintainer note
The empty-string behavior changed intentionally from truthy omission to explicit preservation. If preferred, this can be adjusted to hide empty outputs while still fixing object formatting.

target.title: [codex] feat(ui): render HTML assistant responses in sandboxed iframe
target.body:
## Summary

Complete assistant-generated HTML documents now render in Control UI chat as sandboxed previews instead of raw inline markup. The source stays available in a disclosure, and expanded tool-result HTML uses the same preview path.

AI-assisted by Codex GPT-5.5.

## What Changed

- Added `ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.ts` to detect complete HTML documents, strip whole-message `html`/`htm` fences, cap very large previews, and inject a CSP meta tag into iframe `srcdoc`.
- Updated grouped chat rendering to show previews only for completed assistant messages and expanded tool outputs.
- Added chat preview styles for the iframe, header, truncation label, and source disclosure.
- Added focused tests for detection, CSP injection, truncation, role gates, streaming gates, and tool expansion behavior.

## Scope Boundaries

- Does not preview HTML snippets, prose containing HTML, incomplete documents, or non-whole-message fences.
- Does not preview user, system, developer, or streaming assistant messages.
- Does not change JSON rendering, markdown rendering, collapsed tool output behavior, or existing canvas/embed behavior.

## Security

- The iframe uses an empty `sandbox` attribute, leaving scripts, same-origin access, forms, popups, and top navigation disabled.
- The preview CSP is:

```text
default-src 'none'; navigate-to 'none'; base-uri 'none'; form-action 'none'; img-src data: blob:; media-src data: blob:; font-src data:; style-src 'unsafe-inline'
```

- The renderer uses Lit `.srcdoc` property binding instead of setting a large `srcdoc` attribute.
- Source remains visible under the `Source` disclosure so rendered output is not the only view of the content.

## Verification

Verified on rebased head `f3a017d9a059cceb6f6a10806c0f954cb22f46be` against base `84b025eb622176483ebb1615a88059a4368b3775`.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` passed: 2 files, 74 tests.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.ts ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/styles/chat/text.css` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` produced no output.

Broader local checks were run earlier in the branch:

- `pnpm build` passed.
- `pnpm check` passed.
- A full `pnpm test` run was attempted but hit unrelated local host/environment failures outside this PR's diff.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Complete assistant-generated HTML documents render as sandboxed iframe previews instead of raw inline markup, while the original source remains available in a disclosure.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Chromium through Playwright against current PR head `f3a017d9a059cceb6f6a10806c0f954cb22f46be`; Control UI served by the real Vite dev server using `ui/vite.config.ts`; Gateway traffic supplied by the Control UI E2E harness.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From the PR worktree, ran a `pnpm exec tsx` browser probe that started the Control UI Vite server, loaded `/chat`, installed the E2E Gateway harness with one complete assistant HTML document in history, waited for `.chat-html-preview iframe.chat-html-preview__frame`, and queried the live DOM attributes.
- **Evidence after fix:** Current-head terminal output from the Chromium browser probe:

```json
{
  "head": "f3a017d9a059cceb6f6a10806c0f954cb22f46be",
  "base": "84b025eb622176483ebb1615a88059a4368b3775",
  "previewCount": 1,
  "frameTitle": "Rendered HTML response",
  "sandboxAttribute": "",
  "cspAttribute": "default-src 'none'; navigate-to 'none'; base-uri 'none'; form-action 'none'; img-src data: blob:; media-src data: blob:; font-src data:; style-src 'unsafe-inline'",
  "referrerPolicy": "no-referrer",
  "loading": "lazy",
  "srcdocHasCspMeta": true,
  "srcdocHasNavigateToNone": true,
  "srcdocHasBaseUriNone": true,
  "srcdocHasFormActionNone": true,
  "sourceDisclosurePresent": true,
  "sourceDisclosureOpen": false,
  "sourceTextMatchesOriginalHtml": true,
  "rawHtmlShownAsChatText": false,
  "htmlPreviewElementVisible": true,
  "consoleErrorCount": 0,
  "pageErrorCount": 0
}
```

Supplemental before/after browser artifact from the earlier branch state: https://gist.github.com/wolph/207291436a424d13572b8964821f982e
- **Observed result after fix:** The current-head browser run rendered exactly one visible HTML preview iframe, the iframe sandbox attribute was empty, the final CSP included `navigate-to 'none'`, `base-uri 'none'`, and `form-action 'none'`, the disclosure source matched the original HTML, and the raw doctype markup was not shown as normal chat text.
- **What was not tested:** I did not record a new screenshot/video artifact for the current-head run; the current-head evidence is the copied Playwright/Chromium terminal capture above.

## Review Status

- Copilot review threads were addressed and resolved.
- The remaining merge decision is maintainer acceptance of the sandboxed iframe preview boundary and normal required-check handling.

- Exposes existing workspace-status diagnostics for plugin version drift, plugin compatibility warnings, plugin diagnostics, and TaskFlow recovery hints.
- Keeps the check opt-in/default-off with `defaultEnabled: false`, so plain `doctor --lint` behavior is unchanged.
- Leaves legacy `openclaw doctor` workspace status notes and all real repair behavior unchanged.
- Wires structured lint to the same gated daemon-context `pluginVersionDrift` source used by legacy Doctor output.

## Contract / Safety
- No plugin SDK/public contract changes.
- No config schema, plugin manifest, or persisted data-model changes.
- No new repair path; this is read-only structured detection only.
- Default `openclaw doctor --lint` does not run `core/doctor/workspace-status`; use `--only core/doctor/workspace-status` or `--all` to include it.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Default-disabled structured Doctor lint for workspace status, including selected-lint plugin/status findings and the daemon-context plugin-version-drift path ClawSweeper flagged.

Real environment tested:
WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 source checkout at `073689112ba50fd0d73b341d8b5ce31ff040a897`. Positive plugin-version-drift proof uses the existing source test harness with mocked daemon-status metadata so it can prove the selected lint check receives the same gated `pluginVersionDrift` report as the legacy Doctor path without requiring a live mismatched Gateway. Source CLI zero/default-off proof was run with isolated config/state before this fix and remains applicable to the unchanged default-off behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
2. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.ts src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts`
3. `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.ts src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts`
4. `pnpm tsgo:core`
5. `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check`
6. `pnpm plugin-sdk:check-exports`
7. `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix:

```text
✓ doctor health contributions > keeps workspace status opt-in for structured lint selection
  - default lint: checksRun=0, checksSkipped=1
  - selected lint: checksRun=1, checksSkipped=0
  - collectWorkspaceStatusHealthFindings called with { pluginVersionDrift }

✓ doctor health contributions > passes daemon-context plugin drift into the workspace status note
✓ doctor health contributions > omits daemon-context plugin drift when gateway version used the fallback
✓ doctor health contributions > omits daemon-context plugin drift when probe auth was skipped
✓ doctor health contributions > skips daemon-context plugin drift probes for remote gateway mode

✓ noteWorkspaceStatus > collects plugin version drift as structured findings
✓ noteWorkspaceStatus > surfaces active official managed plugin version drift

Test Files 2 passed
Tests 64 passed
```

Prior source CLI proof for the selected/default-off behavior:

```text
pnpm openclaw doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/workspace-status
exit=0, checksRun=1, checksSkipped=32, findings=0

pnpm openclaw doctor --lint --json
workspaceFindings=0; nonzero exit came from unrelated default checks in the isolated config
```

Observed result after fix:
The selected structured lint check now calls the workspace-status collector with the daemon-context `pluginVersionDrift` report when the same gated legacy conditions are met. Default lint still skips `core/doctor/workspace-status`, remote Gateway mode still avoids local daemon drift probes, and auth-warning/fallback-version cases still suppress drift.

What was not tested:
A live Gateway/plugin-version mismatch environment was not exercised. The positive drift path is covered by source harness proof using mocked daemon-status metadata, plus source CLI proof for selection/default-off behavior.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (2 shards, 64 tests)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.ts src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts`

target.title: fix(anthropic): accept Claude CLI apiKeyHelper auth
target.body:
Fixes #97489

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running Anthropic models through the Claude CLI backend would hit `missing-provider-auth` before `claude` was spawned when their Claude Code auth came from `~/.claude/settings.json` `apiKeyHelper` instead of macOS Keychain or `~/.claude/.credentials.json`.

## Why This Change Was Made

Claude Code documents `apiKeyHelper` as a supported CLI credential source for dynamic credentials, so OpenClaw now treats a non-empty `apiKeyHelper` setting as Claude CLI-managed auth evidence in the shared Claude CLI credential reader. The Anthropic `claude-cli` synthetic auth path uses a non-secret marker for that credential shape; the marker is registered in the bundled Anthropic manifest so generic auth paths do not treat it as plaintext API-key material.

The follow-up commit also closes the status-surface gaps from review: auth labels no longer call helper auth OAuth, doctor reports helper auth as headless Claude auth, external OAuth profile sync still ignores helper-only credentials, and CLI auth epoch coverage proves helper presence participates in local auth-state fingerprinting. OpenClaw does not execute the helper, persist helper output, or expose the marker through generic Anthropic API auth profiles.

## User Impact

Users with proxy, vault, or rotating-key Claude CLI setups can start OpenClaw turns through `agentRuntime.id: "claude-cli"` without creating a dummy `.credentials.json` file just to satisfy OpenClaw's pre-spawn auth check. Status and doctor output now identify helper-backed auth without misleading users into an OAuth-only path.

## Evidence

- Source contract checked: Claude Code docs list `apiKeyHelper` as a custom credential script and include it in authentication precedence: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/iam#credential-management
- Addressed ClawSweeper finding: registered `openclaw:claude-cli-api-key-helper` in `extensions/anthropic/openclaw.plugin.json` `nonSecretAuthMarkers` and added marker coverage in `src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts`.
- Addressed status-surface gaps: added focused coverage in `src/agents/model-auth-label.test.ts`, `src/commands/doctor-claude-cli.test.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts`, and `src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.test.ts`.

target.title: googlechat: deliver media as a text link when attachment upload is unauthorized (app auth)
target.body:
## Summary

When the Google Chat channel is configured with **app authentication** (service account, `chat.bot` scope — the default for most Chat apps), any agent reply that includes media (`MEDIA:<url>`, a markdown image, or the message tool's media param) fails to deliver. The plugin downloads the media and calls the Chat **media upload** API, which returns:

```
403 PERMISSION_DENIED — "Request had insufficient authentication scopes."
```

This is **not a misconfiguration**: per Google's docs, [uploading a file attachment](https://developers.google.com/workspace/chat/upload-media-attachments) is **not supported with app authentication** — it requires *user* auth (domain-wide delegation impersonating a user). App-auth Chat apps simply cannot upload attachments.

Today that 403 fails the whole outbound message. This PR makes both media send paths **fall back to delivering the media URL as a text link** when (a) the upload is rejected for auth/scope reasons and (b) the source is a remote `http(s)` URL — so the user still receives the artifact instead of nothing. Upload behavior is unchanged where it works (user auth / DWD).

## Impact (before this change)

- Any agent/skill that returns media under app auth (e.g. a browser-automation skill returning a screenshot or a downloaded PDF) fails to deliver.
- **Sub-agent completion announces** are hit hardest: the announce errors with `Google Chat upload 403`, retries, then `announce give up (retry-limit)` — and the user gets **no response at all**.
- The artifact is often already a public/temporary URL (e.g. a GCS signed URL) that can simply be shared as a link.

## Repro

1. Google Chat channel with app auth (service account, `chat.bot`).
2. Have an agent reply with `MEDIA:https://<some-public-url>` to a DM.
3. Observe `403 insufficient authentication scopes` on the attachment upload and the message failing to deliver.

## Change

- **`api.ts`** — add `isGoogleChatAttachmentUploadUnauthorized(err)`, which recognizes the 403/scope error (`403` / `PERMISSION_DENIED` / `insufficient authentication scopes`). `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` surfaces these via the shared error path as `Google Chat upload 403: <body>`, so the detector matches the real upload failure.
- **`monitor-reply-delivery.ts`** (`deliverGoogleChatReply`) — the normal channel reply delivery path. Wrap the upload; on an unauthorized error with a remote URL, send the caption + URL as text instead of failing.
- **`channel.adapters.ts`** (outbound adapter `sendMedia`) — the path used by **sub-agent completion announce**. Same fallback, returning a normal text send receipt.
- **`channel.runtime.ts`** — export the new helper so both paths can use it.

```ts
try {
  upload = await uploadGoogleChatAttachment({ account, space, ... });
} catch (err) {
  // App auth (chat.bot) cannot upload attachments (403). For remote URLs,
  // deliver as a text link so the message still reaches the user.
  if (isGoogleChatAttachmentUploadUnauthorized(err) && /^https?:\/\//i.test(mediaUrl)) {
    const linkText = [text, mediaUrl].map((p) => p?.trim()).filter(Boolean).join("\n");
    return await sendGoogleChatMessage({ account, space, text: linkText, thread });
  }
  throw err;
}
```

## Behavior preserved

- **User auth / DWD** deployments still upload native attachments — the fallback only triggers on an auth/scope error.
- **Local-file** media is unaffected (the fallback requires a remote `http(s)` URL).
- **Non-auth** upload errors are re-thrown exactly as before.

## Tests

- `channel.test.ts` → *"falls back to a text link when the attachment upload is unauthorized"* (adapter / announce path).
- `monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` → *"delivers media as a text link when the attachment upload is unauthorized"* (channel reply path).
- Both assert that no attachment is sent and the caption + URL are delivered as text.

Validated against a **production app-auth Chat app**: an agent reply containing media (`MEDIA:<public-url>`) now reaches the user as a clickable link instead of failing with 403 / no delivery.

## Alternatives considered (open to maintainer preference)

- A config flag `channels.googlechat.mediaDelivery: "upload" | "link"` (explicit, skips the upload roundtrip when link delivery is known).
- For images specifically, render a `cardsV2` image widget with `imageUrl` (inline preview, no upload) instead of a bare link — could be a follow-up.



---

## Real behavior proof

Local runs against this branch (post-push head), Node 24 / pnpm 11.2.2, vitest 4.1.7.

### Validation (re-runnable) — all three app-auth upload paths

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts \
  extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts
#  Test Files 3 passed (3)   Tests 30 passed (30)
```
Key passing tests:
```
✓ monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts > delivers media as a text link when the attachment upload is unauthorized   (normal reply path)
✓ channel.test.ts                > falls back to a text link when the attachment upload is unauthorized          (subagent-announce adapter path)
✓ actions.test.ts                > falls back to a text link when the action media upload is unauthorized         (message-action path — NEW)
✓ actions.test.ts                > does not fall back for a local-file upload-file when the upload is unauthorized (re-throw guard — NEW)
```
Type-check: `pnpm tsgo:extensions` + `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` → exit 0.

### Behavior repro (real predicate + authentic 403)

A repro imports the **real** `isGoogleChatAttachmentUploadUnauthorized` from `api.ts` (not a mock copy) and feeds it the authentic 403 string `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` throws (`Google Chat upload 403: {"error":{"code":403,"message":"Request had insufficient authentication scopes.","status":"PERMISSION_DENIED"}}`), then drives the real `deliverGoogleChatReply` with only the network seam mocked:
```
[with-fix]  outbound text = "here is the screenshot\nhttps://storage.example.com/shot.png"
[control-A] NON-403 (500) upload error -> NO fallback, error logged, no link send
[control-B] 403 but NON-https media URL -> NO link fallback
```
**Baseline** (parent commit, pre-fix) with the identical 403 input: the media reply is silently DROPPED (error → log only). **AFTER**: exactly one text message with the URL inlined, no attachment, status sink fired, no runtime error.

### Scope — #89430 fully covered

All three app-auth `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` paths now fall back to a remote-https text link on a 403: `monitor-reply-delivery.ts`, `channel.adapters.ts`, and now `actions.ts` (message-action). The `upload-file` action with a **local** file path intentionally does **not** fall back (the `/^https?:\/\//` guard re-throws) — there is no public URL to post and that action's explicit contract is to attach a file. It is fallback-on-403, not "always link".

Corroboration: on a live openclaw-server (Google Chat app auth) remote media already delivers as a text link with no 403; this repro additionally proves the **403-triggered** branch and its non-regression controls.

target.title: fix(agents,gateway): three subagent announce delivery failures in loopback token-auth setups
target.body:
## Summary

Three related bugs that break subagent completion delivery when running OpenClaw with loopback token-auth (e.g. Google Chat channel, `sessions_spawn` orchestration pattern). All three were found and validated on a production Linux/GCP deployment: OpenClaw 2026.5.20, Node 22, systemd user service.

---

### Fix 1 — `src/gateway/call.ts` — device identity for BACKEND calls with explicit scopes

**Fixes: #77807**

`shouldOmitDeviceIdentityForGatewayCall` unconditionally omitted device identity for all `BACKEND + GATEWAY_CLIENT + loopback` calls. Internal calls such as `callSubagentGateway` carry explicit operator scopes like `["operator.write"]`. Without device identity the gateway cannot verify those scopes against the paired device token and rejects with:

```
Subagent completion direct announce failed: missing scope: operator.write
```

**Fix:** return `false` (keep device identity) when `params.scopes` is non-empty. Also threads `scopes` through `resolveDeviceIdentityForGatewayCall` so the helper can inspect them.

---

### Fix 2 — `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.sessions-yield.ts` — await settle after timeout

`waitForSessionsYieldAbortSettle` raced the settle promise against a 2 s timeout and **returned immediately on timeout**, leaving the session transcript file lock held. The next turn on the same persistent session (e.g. a Google Chat DM) failed with `"file lock stale"`, triggering a model fallback and surfacing an internal error message to end users.

**Fix:** after logging the timeout warning, `await params.settlePromise.catch(() => {})` so the file lock is always released before the function returns.

---

### Fix 3 — `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.sessions-yield.ts` — strip context message before completion announce

When a new incoming message **aborts** an active `sessions_yield`, the `openclaw.sessions_yield` context message (containing `[Context: The previous turn ended intentionally via sessions_yield...]`) remains in the session transcript. When a subagent completion announce subsequently re-runs the agent to deliver the result, the agent sees this context message and responds via `sessions_yield` again (producing a `custom_message`). The announce system does not recognise a `custom_message` as a visible reply and emits:

```
Subagent announce give up: completion agent did not produce a visible reply
```

`stripSessionsYieldArtifacts` already strips the interrupt custom type (`openclaw.sessions_yield_interrupt`) but not the context custom type (`openclaw.sessions_yield`).

**Fix:** strip both types in the in-memory messages loop and in the `fileEntries` loop.

---

## Test plan

- [ ] Single sub-agent turn delivers correctly with no `missing scope: operator.write`
- [ ] Two rapid consecutive messages (second arrives while sub-agent runs): both deliver, no fallback, no stale lock, no scope errors
- [ ] Persistent session (e.g. Google Chat DM) does not accumulate stale locks across turns

Tested on production: Google Chat + Pipedrive sub-agent workflow, 5+ consecutive turns, no errors.

Reported-by: jailbirt <jailbirt@theeye.io>

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)


---

## Real behavior proof — Fix 1 narrowing

Per @martingarramon's review, Fix 1's guard inferred "subagent call" from the mere presence of `params.scopes`, but scopes ride on many ordinary backend calls, so it kept device identity for direct-local shared-token calls too — breaking `call.test.ts:427` and `:658`. Narrowed to an explicit `requireDeviceIdentity` opt-in flag set only at the subagent chokepoint (`callSubagentGateway`).

### Validation (re-runnable)

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-core.config.ts src/gateway/call.test.ts
#  Test Files 1 passed (1)   Tests 88 passed (88)   (incl :427, :658, + new regression test)

# full checks-node-agentic-gateway-core shard:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-core.config.ts
#  Test Files 131 passed (131)   Tests 1957 passed (1957)

# full agentic-control-plane-auth-node shard (25 files from scripts/lib/ci-node-test-plan.mjs):
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts <…>
#  Test Files 25 passed (25)   Tests 279 passed (279)
```
Both previously-failing shards now pass. New regression test: a loopback BACKEND scoped call with `requireDeviceIdentity: true` keeps `deviceIdentity` (scopes `["operator.write"]`, BACKEND/GATEWAY_CLIENT).

### Change

- `src/gateway/call.ts` — add `requireDeviceIdentity?: boolean` to `CallGatewayBaseOptions`; in `shouldOmitDeviceIdentityForGatewayCall` replace `if (requestedScopes.length > 0) return false;` with `if (params.opts.requireDeviceIdentity === true) return false;`; drop the now-unused `scopes` arg from `resolveDeviceIdentityForGatewayCall`.
- `src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts` — `callSubagentGateway` passes `requireDeviceIdentity: true` (the single chokepoint the completion announce / `agent` call flows through).
- `src/gateway/call.test.ts` — regression test reproducing the original bug.

### Cross-PR #85424

Both touch `executeGatewayRequestWithScopes` but on non-overlapping regions: #85424 adds event-loop-drift/timeout handling in the body; this PR's only edit there is the `resolveDeviceIdentityForGatewayCall(...)` argument at the deviceIdentity line. No semantic conflict — suggest #85424 merges first and this rebases on top.

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).
